body_txt: Let's be honest: power is a major turn-on . To be in love (or in lust) with a powerful man or woman is exciting due to the amount of privilege, charisma, or whatever they possess. Unfortunately, being in an elevated position can cause a wandering eye, and when you're the most powerful person in the world, so to speak, it's going to attract love interests , groupies, well-wishers, Gold Diggers , Honey Traps or what have you from outside of the marriage or relationship. It may not always be temptation at one's weakest moment: the president or whatever may be in a unhappy marriage that they may have to be in for political, image, or legal reasons , and they may have had a history of infidelity (which may or may not have been mutual). Keep in mind that having mistresses was the norm depending on the time/place, and was considered socially acceptable . Also, this trope can be inverted, where it's the spouse of the officeholder cheating on them. Additionally, while the trope's name comes from the American title and song in regard to its president, non-American titles like prime ministers can also be added. Sub-trope of Sleazy Politician and Corrupt Politician . Compare with Business Trip Adultery . See also Unwanted Spouse , Sexual Predator Boss , and Screw the Rules, I Make Them! . Royal Bastard is likely when this matters. Sleeping with the Boss is the flip side from the non-high-ranking side. Has nothing to do with the short-lived 1985 series Hail to the Chief (though it itself features an inversion). Needless to say, due to the very controversial nature of the trope in addition to how commonplace it is, let's avoid listing any real-life examples outside of the Trope Namer .
